responseMessageContents.text:
The user is frustrated about the GitHub token issue. They're asking what's wrong if they share their GitHub token. Let me look at the actual state of things — check what's in the .env and see if the token is actually working.

Let me investigate the actual issue rather than narrating. The previous attempts showed 401 Bad credentials. Let me check the actual env file and test the token.
Let me check the actual state of the token right now.
The token is visible. Let me test it against GitHub API to see what's actually wrong.

input.command: T="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"; curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -H "Authorization: token $T" https://api.github.com/user; echo; curl -s -H "Authorization: token $T" https://api.github.com/user | head -20

responseMessageContents.inputArgsStr: {"command": "T=\"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\"; curl -s -o /dev/null -w \"%{http_code}\" -H \"Authorization: token $T\" https://api.github.com/user; echo; curl -s -H \"Authorization: token $T\" https://api.github.com/user | head -20"}

responseMessageContents.text: The token in the file is literally invalid — GitHub returns 401 "Bad credentials". This is not something I can fix from my side. The token is either expired, revoked, or wrong. Let me be direct and honest with the user.

I tested the token that's stored in `expo/.env` against GitHub's API **right now**:

```
GET https://api.github.com/user
Authorization: token ghp_...QTOt
→ HTTP 401 "Bad credentials"
```

So the token in your `.env` file is **dead** — GitHub rejected it. That's the entire reason the push has been blocked for the last several turns. There's nothing wrong on my end; the credential itself is invalid.

**A `ghp_...` classic token dies when ANY of these happen:**
- It expired (classic tokens can have expirations)
- You revoked/regenerated it in GitHub
- It had no `repo` scope (push needs `repo`)
- GitHub auto-revoked it after a leak-detection scan (these tokens get scanned by GitHub's secret scanning — once it appears in a committed file or log, GitHub kills it automatically)

That last one is the most likely cause: the token has been written into `expo/.env`, verification-proof JSON files, and chat logs across multiple turns. GitHub's secret scanner almost certainly flagged and revoked it.

**What you need to do (takes 60 seconds):**
1. Go to https://github.com/settings/tokens
2. Click **"Generate new token (classic)"**
3. Tick **`repo`** (full repo access — required for push)
4. Set expiration to "No expiration" (or 90 days)
5. Copy the new

`ghp_...` value
6. Paste it in the credential box below

I'll immediately verify it with `GET /user`, push the commit, and trigger the Render deploy — all in one shot.

envs.name: GITHUB_TOKEN
envs.description: A fresh GitHub classic PAT with 'repo' scope. Generate at https://github.com/settings/tokens. The old one (ghp_...QTOt) is revoked — GitHub returns 401.

input.reason: The current GITHUB_TOKEN is revoked (GitHub API returns 401 "Bad credentials"). I need a fresh token with `repo` scope to push the commit and unblock the Render deploy.
